title: Optimized ERC20 transferFrom with allowance check and Permit2 infinite allowance fast path
explanation: Key optimizations: (1) shl(96, from) packs the address into upper bits, reused across multiple slot computations saving repeated mstore calls, (2) if not(allowance_) checks for max uint256 in one opcode skipping the decrement entirely for infinite allowances, (3) allowance and balance slots both derived using the packed from_ avoiding redundant address operations. Note: the actual Solady source has two code paths — the Permit2-enabled branch additionally checks eq(caller(), _PERMIT2) to skip the allowance check entirely. The YUL shown here represents the non-Permit2 branch logic.
when_to_apply: Any ERC20 transferFrom with standard allowance mapping
when_not_to_apply: Non-standard allowance logic, permit-only tokens, tokens where max allowance should be decremented
